questions: Why is the man returning the sweater?
What will the woman most likely do next?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Hi. I need to return this sweater. I bought it yesterday, but it's too small for me.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Sure. Do you have the receipt?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yes, I have it right here.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Great. We can process the return. Do you want to exchange it for a larger size or get a refund?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'd like to exchange it, please.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: No problem. I will get another in that color for you from our stock.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Thank you.

questions: What is the man doing later today?
What is the woman likely to do next?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Do you have time to do me a favor?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'm about to run to the post office, but I can help you after that.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I'm hosting this dinner party tonight and I need to pick up some last minute groceries.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: What do you still need?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I need vegetables and ingredients for a dessert. Here's a list.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: No problem. I can swing by the market after I've mailed my package.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: You're a lifesaver. Thanks a million.

questions: What are the man and the woman talking about?
What does the man imply when he says, "I'll bring a blanket too"?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Listen to a conversation

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Have you heard about the concert that the university orchestra is putting on in the park? I know you're a big fan of classical music.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I am. And I'm already going with a group of friends. You should come with us.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Thanks, I'd love to. So how much are tickets?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You only have to pay if you sit in the seats up close near the stage. We're all going to bring blankets and just sit on the grass a little farther back.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Good idea. I'll bring a blanket too.

questions: What is the man's problem?
At the end of the conversation, what is the man's attitude toward the woman's idea?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I was just typing my paper and my computer suddenly shut down.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You've had a lot of problems with that laptop, haven't you?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried holding down the power button for a few seconds?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I did, but nothing happened. I'd rather not take it to a repair shop, but I don't know what else to do.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: How about you get in touch with my ex roommate, Monica? She's a real tech whiz.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Oh, that's an idea. Could you give me her number? I'm not sure I have it anymore.

questions: What can be inferred about the bakery?
Based on the conversation, what will happen during the interview?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: This is Jimmy Clerken from Rise Bakery calling. I received a message that you'd like to do a story about my shop for your university newspaper.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Yes. You've been a staple in our city for generations now. The grandparents of our current students were customers there when they were young.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's a family owned business and we're proud of it. Would you be able to come next Monday at 3 o'clock?

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Definitely. Is it all right if my photographer comes along?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Of course. You can get different shots, even back in our kitchen where we do all the baking.

A Have you heard about the concert that the university orchestra is putting on in the park? I know you're a big fan of classical music. B: I am and I'm already going with a group of friends. You should come with us. A: Thanks, I'd love to, so how much your tickets? B: You only have to pay if you sit in the seats up close near the stage. We're all going to bring blankets and just sit on the grass a little farther back. A: Good idea, I'll bring a blanket too.

A: Hi, I received a parking ticket at the Smith Street parking garage yesterday. It said I was parked in an area reserved for faculty. B: Were you parked on the first level? A: Yes, but I've been parking there for months now. I thought it was a student area. B: It was, but we changed it last week. There are signs posted outside the garage. A: Oh honestly, I probably wasn't paying attention anyway. Can I go ahead and pay it now?

A: I need to buy a birthday present for my friend who loves cooking. Do you have any ideas? B: How about a high quality set of kitchen knives? A: That's a good idea, but I think she has a good set of knives already. She cooks a lot so she has all the basic equipment. B: Maybe you should stop by the Kitchenware store downtown and ask their advice. I bet they have a lot of fun stuff for people who already have a well stocked kitchen.

A: Did you hear about the mural project our universities doing? I really think you should enter. B: Wait, what mural project? C: They want to create a mural on the side of the student center. Students will submit paintings and the judges will select the top five, and then those students will lead the official mural committee. A: Are you doing it? The paintings you've done in our class are amazing. B: I already have the newspaper club and student council. A:Well, it sounds interesting. I'll look into it.

items.setting: Listen to a conversation? A: Wasn't there supposed to be a yoga class in the conference room at noon today? I stopped in right at noon and no one was there.

conversation.text: I was really looking forward to it. B: Didn't you get the e-mail? The instructor had a personal conflict and had to cancel. A: When did she send it? I don't know, maybe 11:15? B: That makes sense. I've been away from my desk since mid morning and haven't checked my e-mail. A: Then you probably don't know that she is rescheduled for tomorrow.

I just interviewed the director of campus housing for the university newspaper, and she confirmed the rumors. The cost to live in the dormitories will be about 10% higher next year. Oh, wow. That's a lot. I'm wondering if it'd make sense to start looking for an apartment or something that's not on campus. That's what I'm planning to do. If I come across any good ones I'm not taking, I'll pass them along to you. Awesome. Thanks.

Have you heard about the concert that the university orchestra is putting on in the park? I know you're a big fan of classical music. I am. And I'm already going with a group of friends. You should come with us. Thanks, I'd love to. So how much are tickets? You only have to pay if you sit in the seats up close near the stage. We're all going to bring blankets and just sit on the grass a little farther back. Good idea. I'll bring a blanket too.

Hi, this is Sarah calling from the campus tutoring center. I just wanted to confirm your biology session with Raul tomorrow at noon. I'm glad you called. My manager at work needs me to go in tomorrow. I get off at three. Hmm. Okay, let me check the schedule. Raoul's not available at that time, but we could put you in with Kira at 3:30. That'd be perfect. I've worked with her before and she's great. Thank you.

Hi, Ben. Did you get your laptop issue all sorted out? Yeah, I called the IT desk, but couldn't get an appointment till next weekend, so I took it to the place down the street. The technician replaced the faulty ram and it's working great now. Awesome. Did it take a long time? I had it back the same day. The price wasn't bad and the people were super friendly. That's good to know. Yeah. I'll text you the name of the place in case you ever need it.

Are you planning to attend the theater performance this Friday? I am, but I've had so much to do about moving to my new apartment this week that I haven't even had time to look into buying my ticket yet. You should hurry. It's selling out fast. I'll buy my ticket today. The play got some rave reviews, so I'd hate to miss out on it.

Hi, Emily. Have you had any luck with your search for a roommate for your new apartment? Not yet. I've posted ads online, but I haven't received many responses. Have you tried asking around at the university? Your classmates might know someone. That's a good idea. I'll ask around tomorrow. I hope you find someone soon. It's so expensive when you're not sharing the rent. Definitely.

Have you booked your flight for the conference yet? Not yet. Experience tells me that if I wait long enough, I can get a great last minute deal. What happens if there are no tickets left though? You might miss the conference. That's never been a problem before. But I suppose there's always a first time. And I'm meeting professors there to discuss my research. I think you're right. I'd better go get a ticket now.

I'm looking for a good restaurant to celebrate finishing my thesis. Do you have any recommendations? How about that new French restaurant downtown? I went last week and I had a chicken dish that was delicious. That sounds perfect. My advisor loves French food. I'll check if they have any availability for Friday night. You might want to call them soon. I think they fill up fast.

My printer isn't working and I have to print this report now. What's the rush? I have a class at 3 o'clock. Have you checked if it's out of paper or ink? Yes, I did. The paper tray is full and I replaced the ink cartridge yesterday. Maybe it's a software issue. You could try reinstalling the printer driver. That often resolves these kinds of problems. Or you can call the help desk. That will take forever, and it's almost 3 o'clock. I guess I'll just use the printer in the computer lab.

Did you print a copy of your resume for the interview tomorrow? I didn't need to. I submitted it online. That was what they requested. Okay. I had to bring several copies of mine when I applied for that internship, and I met with several people for the interview. What was the position again? In the accounting department. Maybe it's different. In graphic design, I'm only meeting with one person. Oh, that sounds a lot less stressful.

A: Listen to a conversation. B: Hey, Linda. I've been looking all over for you. A: I just got back from lunch. What's up? B: I've been thinking about getting a new couch for my dorm room. Do you have any recommendations? A: Have you checked out Comfort Furniture Store? I shop there all the time. They have a great selection and their prices are reasonable. B: Do you know if they offer delivery services? A: Yes, they do, and they're having a sale starting tomorrow. I'll send you a link to their catalog.

B: Listen to a conversation. A: Are Alyssa and David back from studying abroad in Hong Kong? B: Yes, they returned last week. Alyssa's still adjusting to the time difference. A: I can relate. I had bad jet lag last summer after traveling across the globe. I kept taking long naps, which affected my sleep at night. B: Yeah, instead of napping, getting outside for a walk and sunlight helps reset your internal clock. I told Alyssa the same. A: Once she's over it, let's all have lunch. B: Sounds good. She just needs to sort out credit for an extra course she took over there, then she'll be free.

questions: Why does the woman ask about maintenance on her car?
What will the woman most likely do next?

conversation.text: A: I'm planning a trip to visit my aunt during spring break. What maintenance should I do on my car? B: Make sure to check the tire pressure and oil level. It's also a good idea to have your car inspected by a mechanic before you go. A: That's helpful, Can you recommend a mechanic? There's a garage on Court Street across from the grocery store. I'll give you a link to book an appointment. B: Thanks. I'll take care of that right away.

questions: Why was the man looking for the woman?
What does the woman suggest she will do in the future?

conversation.text: A: I just heard that the art store in the mall is going out of business. B: Oh, no. I get painting supplies for my senior art project there. A: I know. That's why I came looking for you. What will you do now? B: Well, I have a couple of options. I could get them online or I can go to the store in Porterville, 10 miles away. A: Seems to me like you'd want to buy that kind of stuff in person. B: I would. It looks like I'll be making regular trips to Porterville.

questions: What does the woman like about the apartment?
What does the woman say she will do tomorrow?

conversation.text: A: Hey Josie, how's the apartment hunt going? B: I'm so glad you asked. I think I found the one. It's close to campus, which is nice. A: That's awesome. You'll be near the university? That'll be convenient. Did you sign the lease yet? B: Not yet. I'm meeting with the landlord tomorrow. I'll tour the grounds and see the pool too. A: Good luck. I'll keep my fingers crossed.

questions: Why is the man taking a train to the conference?
What does the man suggest that the woman do?

conversation.text: A: Did you say you're taking the train to the conference instead of driving? B: That's right. I'm sure I'll still be working on my conference paper and it will be nice to have some quiet time on the train to complete it. A: That's smart. I was going to drive. B: That's not a bad idea either. That way you'll have your car for getting around while you're there. A: Yes, but driving alone is boring. Maybe I'll take the train too. B: Well be sure to bring a book if you do. 2 hours is a long time on the train.

questions: What does the man imply about a restaurant?
What does the man imply when he mentions a bed?

conversation.text: A: Have you checked out the new restaurant downtown? The sushi is made by an award-winning chef and is absolutely top notch. B: I'd love to, but I'm saving money for a vacation. A: Do you have specific plans? B: Not yet, but I'd love to spend a month somewhere in nature. A: Have you considered a camping trip? You could just pack a tent and a sleeping bag into your car and travel all around the mountains while sleeping at campsites. No need for hotels or plane tickets. B: Well, I do love me a comfortable bed at the end of an eventful day.

A: Hi, Maya. Since today's your first day with us at the Health Center, I'll be training you. Have you ever worked as a receptionist before? B: Yeah, I had a similar job at the campus Health Center at my old university, but only on the weekends. A: Great. OK, so we have this checklist here of items we have to go through. It's rather comprehensive and we won't get to everything today. It's just been updated so it should be pretty easy to use. B: I'm eager to get started.

A: I saw the new painting exhibition in the Art Museum. Have you? B: Moving to my new apartment has taken up all of my free time recently. There's so much packing and unpacking to do. How's the exhibit? A: There's a good variety of paintings and their arrangement is really creative. I just wish it featured some stuff from artists in our area. We have so many talented painters here whose work is worth seeing, but All in all it's quite awesome. A: I'll go check it out this weekend before it closes. I should be all moved in by then.

A: Have you tried using that new app our physics professor mentioned? I was going to download it, but then I figured I wouldn't have time to figure out how to use it. B: I can totally show you. It's not that bad, and it's been helpful for our last couple of homework assignments. A: Great, I was planning to go to the tutoring canter for extra help. B: You probably won't need to now. Here, I'll show you where to get it. And I have the code the professor gave us for the free trial. A: I actually have that.

A: Did you end up seeing that movie yesterday with your friend? B: Yes. It was a good way to spend some time with her. It was an afternoon showing and the place was practically empty. A: How was the movie? B: It was OK. There were lots of unexpected twists, but it was too long. A: Should I see it? B: I'm not sure I'd pay money to see it in the theater. Just wait a few months and it will be out on streaming. A: That's usually how I watch movies these days anyway.

A: So did you manage to get those tickets for the theatre? B: Yes, I got some great seats near the stage for Friday evening. A: That's fantastic. What play did you say it is? B: It's a modern adaptation of Shakespeare's King Lear. A: Oh, that sounds interesting. B: I think so too. I read King Lear in high school, but I've never seen it performed and I find the idea of a modern adaptation really intriguing. I sure hope my sister does too.

A: I'm so excited about our road trip this weekend. I'm all packed and got the food and snacks for the trip. B: I thought David was supposed to prepare snacks. A: No, it was me. David signed up to bring water for everyone. B: I see. A: Are you all packed? B: Yes, I just want to get my car checked before we leave. I'm taking it to the mechanic tomorrow morning. A: Good idea by the way. I just checked and there might be light showers in the afternoon, but overall good weather on our route.

A: Customer support. How may I help you? B: Well my computer keeps overheating and shutting down. A: Have you checked if the fans are working properly? Dust can accumulate and block air flow. B: I haven't looked at that. A: You can get a can of compressed air and spray it into the vents and the fans to blow out the dust. B: OK I'll try that. A: Hopefully that'll take care of the problem. If not, you'll probably need to ship it to us so we can take a look.

A: Welcome to the student housing office. What can I do for you? B: Hi, I'm looking for a 2 bedroom apartment and I wondered if you have some available? A: Yes we do. How much is the rent? B: Standard is $1800 a month but we also have some with a nice view of the lake. Those are 2100 hundred. A: 21 hundred is a bit much. Do you mind if I have a look at one of the standard 2 bedrooms? B: Of course. Let me grab the key from the drawer.

A: I'm so grateful to you for looking after my dog while I'm in the mountains. B: I'm happy I can help. How's the planning for your trip coming? A: I've already booked a flight and a cabin. All I need now is to arrange a rental car. B: Terrific. How long are you staying? A: I'll be in the cabin for one week and then I'll spend another week hiking a trail in the National Park. B: Wow, my vacations are so boring. I really envy you.

A: I bought a traditional recipe book and started learning how to bake. B: That sounds great. How has it been going so far? A: Well, with mixed success, My chocolate cake turned out fabulous, But my cookies, well, let's say I'm not going to have anyone try them. I managed to eat a couple, but only because I have strong teeth. B: You'll do better next time. A: I hope so. In fact, I'm going to try again tonight. I'm headed to the store right now to get some extra flour and sugar.

A: Did you go to the rock concert in the park yesterday afternoon? B: I went to a gardening workshop at the University Center instead. It's sad that I missed out on the concert, but I'm starting my own garden and I need to know all the insurance and outs of growing herbs and vegetables. A: Nice. And to be honest, you didn't miss that much, B: no? A: Well, I love some of the songs they played, but the acoustics in the park aren't very good, so the sound quality was rather mediocre and didn't do justice to the songs.

A: How was the photography workshop last week? B: We learned techniques for taking portraits. Next week we'll do action photography. A: That sounds so interesting. I've always wanted to take pictures of tennis matches. B: The instructor who will lead the class next week has photographed state championships in six different sports. A: I'd love to attend. I'll buy a new camera for the occasion. Are all ability levels welcome? B: Yes, I'll e-mail you the link to sign up. Just be aware that spots are going fast.

A: Are you going to the outdoor concert this Saturday? B: Is it still happening with all the rain in the forecast? A: They said it's on, rain or shine. Plus we might get a dry window in the evening. B: Hmm. I'll think about it. I have a lot of studying to do for my law school exam, but I'd love to see the performance of the headline band, the Rumbling Waves. I grew up in the same neighborhood as their lead singer and even played basketball with him a few times.

A: Is that the subway schedule you're looking at? Is Line 1 running on time? B: It says there might be a delay due to track maintenance. A: Oh no, I hope it's not too long. I have to be at campus by 10 to present and turn in the biology project report. B: I recommend taking an earlier train just to be safe. The delays can be up to an hour. A: Good idea, I'll head that way now.

A: Should we take the train or fly to the conference? B: The train is slower, but the train station is a quick walk from the conference hotel. The airport is at least half an hour away. A: Train it is then. I hope the hotel has nice views of the city. B: That I can't answer. Just be ready to leave at 7 AM. A: 7:00. That's awfully early. Is that the only option? B: I thought we should take an early train so we have time to sightsee when we get there.

A: Hi Lisa, Do you have any plans for the weekend? B: Well, I did want to go to a baseball game in Center City on Saturday, but the weather's not looking too good. A: Yes, storms are forecast all weekend, I think. B: Right, So I decided to go to that new art gallery in town this week. Better to do something indoors. A: Good thinking. I went last week and there were a few sculptures by a local artist that really caught my eye. You need to check them out. B: Oh, nice tip. I can't wait to see them.

A: Are you excited about the music festival this Saturday? Neon Chapel is performing and they're one of my favorite bands. B: Mine too, but my parents are going to be visiting for the weekend and it seems they're not interested. A: Oh I see. B: Yeah, it was a last minute thing. A few of my paintings are on display at the campus Art Museum right now for the student exhibit and they want to see them. A: Oh well, that'll be fun. And you should take your parents to the Italian place on Front St. it's really good.

questions: Why does the woman need to create a budget?
What does the man suggest the woman do with her list of expenses?

conversation.text: B: How's your business class going? Have you been given any assignments yet? A: First one's due on my. Monday. I'm supposed to create a budget for an imaginary company. I've come up with a list of expenses, but I don't know what to do next. B: Now that you've listed your expenses, you should prioritize them. A: That makes sense. There probably won't be enough revenue at first to cover all of my costs. B: That's exactly right. And don't forget to include a contingency fund. You can bet that unexpected costs will arise sooner or later. A: This is a great start. Thanks.

questions: What about the woman surprises the man?
What does the man say he will do tomorrow?

conversation.text: A: I was just over at the student center, and they were showing this awesome documentary about the wildlife in our region. B: I know. I was part of the film crew. You were? We were all sworn to secrecy until the project was completed. I'm glad you liked it. A: Like it? I loved it. It was one of the most amazing programs I've ever seen. The photography was stunning. I'll definitely be there tomorrow night when they show it again.

questions: Why is the man seeking advice?
What does the man imply when he says, "I'll keep a full pitcher of water at my desk at all

conversation.text: A: I've been having trouble concentrating when I study. Do you have any advice? B: Have you tried taking short breaks throughout the day? That can help refresh your mind. A: I usually just keep working until I'm finished with the assignment. Maybe I should try that. B: Also make sure you're staying hydrated and getting enough sleep. Both can greatly affect your concentration. A: I'll keep a full pitcher of water at my desk at all times. times"?

items.setting: Listen to a conversation A: Have you finished that book you were reading on photography techniques.
questions: Why does the man mention practical tips?
What does the man imply he will do?

conversation.text: B; Almost. It's really insightful. A: I've been thinking about picking up photography as a hobby. Do you recommend the book for beginners? B: Definitely. It covers the basics very well and has plenty of practical tips. A: You think they have it at the campus bookstore? Maybe I'll pick up a copy. B: Yeah, that's where I got it. But next week's your birthday, and I haven't gotten you a gift yet, so maybe just wait until then.

questions: What kind of painting did the woman complete?
What does the woman plan to do with her painting?

conversation.text: A: Hi, Lisa. Did you finish your painting? B: Yes, I completed it yesterday. It's a watercolor of a mountain lake at sunrise. Lots of soft colors and reflections. I think it came out well. A: That sounds beautiful. Are you planning to display it anywhere? B: I'm thinking of entering it in the university art show next month. I've never submitted anything before so I'm a little nervous. A: I think you should go for it, it sounds like a piece people would really enjoy. B: Thanks, I hope so. I just need to get it framed first.

A: I've been trying to get in shape, but I'm not having much success know a lot about Fitness. What am I doing wrong? B: The most important thing is staying motivated. A: Yes, that's been a problem for me. B: You should set small. Achievable goals. Seeing your progress can be very motivating. A: I see what you're saying. B: You might also want to think about joining a fitness class, working out with others. Can keep you motivated and accountable. A: That's a great idea. I'll go online now and see what's available.

A: I've been put in charge of food for the University, football teams holiday party, next week. And I don't know where to start. B: How many people will be there? A: Around 40. B: That's a big group. The first thing you need to do is find out people's likes and dislikes and see if anyone has any dietary restrictions. A: That makes sense. How do I go about it? B: Just write up a little questionnaire and send it to everyone. One then based on the responses, right? Up a menu and go shopping. A: Nice. I'll work on that now.

A: My computer keeps freezing whenever I try to open a large file. B: Have you tried restarting it? A: Yes, but it didn't help, I'm worried, it might be a hardware issue. B: Maybe it's time for a memory upgrade that could solve the problem. A: You might be right. I'll take it to a technician to check. B: Good idea. They'll be able to help you.

A: I've been thinking about taking up photography. B; Really. Why photography? A: A friend of mine is really into it and it sounds interesting. B: Just be sure not to spend too much money until you know you want to stick with it. Trust me when I say that photography is an expensive hobby. A: Do you have any tips for a beginner? B: Start by learning the basics of your camera and practicing different techniques. A: That makes sense. Any good online resources? B: There are plenty of free tutorials. You can also join photography forums for feedback and advice from others.

A; Hello, I ordered some school supplies on your website but my classes start Monday and I'm nervous the items won't arrive on time. B: Well, we do offer an expedited option. If you placed your order in the last 24 hours I can still update it. A: Oh that would be great. How much extra is it ? B: $20.00 but you can also sign up for an annual membership $5 a month for unlimited expedited shipping. A: All right. I guess I'll do that then.

A: Hi Lisa, have you managed to set up the new software on your computer? B: Not yet. I tried installing it but I keep getting an error message. A: Did you check if your system meets the software requirements? B: Yes, my computer is definitely compatible so I think it might be a network issue. A: You should contact IT support, they can help you sort it out. B: Good idea. I'll give them a call this afternoon. A: Let me know how it goes.

A: I'm so excited for the outdoor concert this weekend. B: Have you seen the weather forecast? We get soaked. A: Well that's disappointing. I was really looking forward to going. B: Same here. Any thoughts on what to do instead? I A: heard there's a new exhibit opening on Saturday at the art gallery. Tickets go on sale tomorrow. B: Count me in. We can meet up for breakfast that morning too.

A: What do you know about this rock festival that's happening? B: Not much. Do you know what time it starts? A: It kicks off at 3:00 PM. B: That's perfect. Oh, I remember hearing that. There will be food trucks. A: Oh, right. And a dance floor. B: No way. You can't keep us away. Let's get there early so we can get a good spot.

A: Have you heard they're finally updating the kitchen in the student lounge of this dormitory? B: It's about time. What are they doing? A: Well, for one, all of the appliances are being replaced. B: Oh, that's great. The microwave hasn't been working properly for a while. A: I heard they're replacing some of the cabinets too. B: I hope it doesn't take too long. I cook there a lot. A: Me too. I think we'll have access to the place in the dorm next door though.

A: Lisa, you've been taking guitar lessons, right? How's that going? B: It's good. I'm having fun and there are four or five songs I can play pretty well now. A: I've been thinking about it myself. I just finished a big work project so I've got some spare time. B: If you'll wait here a minute I'll give you my teachers information. She's great. A: Do you do it online or in person? B: I took a few lessons in person to get started. It's helpful to have someone show you some basic things, but now we do it online and it works well.

A: I saw your photo of a mother duck with her chicks in the student magazine. That was amazing. B: Oh, thanks. I was lucky to get that close to them. A: Is photography a hobby for you? B: Sort of. It's something I enjoy in my free time, but I don't have much of that. A: You should join the college photography club. We have a street photography workshop coming up that should be really interesting. B: I prefer wildlife, but that does sound like a lot of fun. Can you send me a link to the club's website?

B: My parents are visiting the city this weekend. I'll show them the new art museum and the botanical gardens. Nice. I'm still trying to decide where to take them for dinner. A: How about that new seafood restaurant by the waterfront? Their food is really fresh. Whatever the local fishers catch that day. B: Wow. A: And the view is spectacular, especially at sunset. Hey, how was the concert last night? I don't know. I couldn't get in. I must have dropped my ticket on the bus or something on my way there. Wait, you had a printed ticket? Usually now people just get them on their phones. Right. Well, my phone battery needs to be replaced. And I was worried my phone would die. So I just bought a paper ticket from the box office. I had it with me when I left my house. But when I got there, it was gone. How disappointing

I'm thinking about switching to a vegetarian diet. Do you have any tips? Definitely. Start by incorporating more vegetables and plant-based proteins into your meals. That sounds doable. I've heard tofu is a good option. Yes, tofu is great. You can also try beans and nuts for protein. Thanks for the advice. I'll give it a try

I was training a new employee yesterday, and I noticed that several pieces of info in the documents we use don't reflect our current practices here in the computer lab. Would you be able to help me update everything and print new copies? Sure. Do you want to work on it together or split it up so you do half and I do half? Which do you think will be faster? We have three new staff members starting next week, so we should have it done by then. Let's divide and conquer then

Have you tried the yoga class at the University Recreation No, I haven't. But I've been meaning to. I really like the morning class. It's at 8am with an instructor named Julie. I'll look into it. The only issue involves space. They have us packed so tightly in that room that you can barely move. Well, they must offer more than one session, right? Yeah, there's an evening one too. But it meets at the same time as my biology seminar

Standard is $1,000 a month, but we also have some with a nice view of the lake. Those are 2100 2100 is a bit much Do you mind if I have a look at one of the standard two bedrooms? Of course, let me grab the key from the drawer

I've been trying to get in shape, but I'm not having much success You know a lot about fitness. What am I doing wrong? The most important thing is staying motivated Yes, that's been a problem for me. You should set small achievable goals Seeing your progress can be very motivating. I see what you're saying You might also want to think about joining a fitness class Working out with others can keep you motivated and accountable. That's a great idea. I'll go online now and available

I heard you join the campus chess club. How's it going? Great. I'm learning a lot and meeting new people I've always wanted to learn to play, but I've never I did is everyone pretty advanced No, not necessarily. There are members of all skill levels and everyone's very supportive When do they meet? Every Saturday afternoon at the Student Center Okay, I usually work at the library then, but they're very flexible. So I'm sure I could make it work Listen to a time archaeology podcast Throughout the years Archaeologists have discovered many fascinating artifacts on the Greek island of Crete one example is the Feistos disk an Ancient clay artifact that was found in the ruins of the Minoan palace at Feistos the Minoan civilization lasted from about 3100 BCE to 1100 CE and we think this artifact was made in the second millennium BCE it was discovered in 1908 and it features many stamped symbols 241 to be exact in the shape of a spiral the symbols They include images of things like animals tools and so on These animals tools and other images form a pattern in the shape of a spiral What's so interesting is that these symbols are unlike anything else from the Minoanization or any other known writing system Many experts have tried to decipher these enigmatic symbols Some suggest they could represent an unknown language Others think they might represent a calendar or even a religious hymn yet No consensus has been reached Another intriguing thing about the Feistos disk is the method used to imprint the symbols This involved pressing prefabricated stamps into wet clay This process is really remarkable. It suggests a level of sufficient similar to early printing Some scholars hypothesize that the disk might be an import from another culture a lone artifact brought to Crete by traders or travelers But this is another point of contention surrounding this fascinating artifact

I'm so grateful to you for looking after my dog while I'm in the mountains. I'm happy I can help. How's the planning for your trip coming? I've already booked a flight and a cabin. All I need now is to arrange a rental car. Terrific. How long are you staying? I'll be in the cabin for one week and then I'll spend another week hiking a trail in the national park. Wow. My vacations are so boring. I really envy you

Have you heard about the concert that the university orchestra is putting on in the park? I know you're a big fan of classical music. I am. And I'm already going with a group of friends. You should come with us. Thanks. I'd love to. So how much are tickets? You only have to pay if you sit in the seats up close near the stage. We're all going to bring blankets and just sit on the grass a little farther back. Good idea. I'll bring a blanket too

I think we need to meet again to discuss the group project. Definitely. Still a lot to talk about. Are you free Thursday afternoon? We can meet at the cafe near the library. Does Friday work for you? I don't have classes on Thursdays, so I'm not planning to come over to campus. Sure, but I thought you lived in a dorm on campus. I did, but I just moved last weekend. I still have lots of boxes piled up in my new apartment. Moving is always a lot of work

I missed the online art history class this morning. Did you hop on? Yes. The lecture focused on Renaissance architecture, especially Brunelleschi's dome in Florence. It was fascinating. That sounds interesting. Was there a discussion afterward? There was supposed to be, but the professor's internet cut out halfway through. That's too bad. Yeah, I had a few questions about the construction that I wanted to clarify. I posted them in the class forum, though. Well, maybe the professor's seen them by now

You went on the art history trip to Italy over spring break, right? How was it? It was great. We visited so many amazing churches and art museums. Professor Douglas from the Art History Department led the group. He's great, but, well, a few times we heard lectures from Italian professors. It was so cool to hear about art history from a local. I wish there had been more of that. I'm thinking about doing the program next year. Well, don't wait until the last minute. There's quite a bit involved in the application process

So did you manage to get those tickets for the theatre? Yes, I got some great seats near the stage for Friday evening. That's fantastic. What play did you say it is? It's a modern adaptation of Shakespeare's King Lear. Oh, that sounds interesting. I think so too. I read King Lear in high school, but I've never seen it performed, and I find the idea of a modern adaptation really intriguing. I sure hope my sister does too

Welcome to the student housing office. What can I do for you? Hi. I'm looking for a two-bedroom apartment, and I wondered if you have some available. Yes, we do. How much is the rent? Standard is $1,800 a month, but we also have some with a nice view of the lake. Those are $2,100. $2,100 is a bit much. Do you mind if I have a look at one of the standard two bedrooms? Of course. Let me grab the key from the drawer

So have you finished the gardening book? My roommate wants to borrow it next. I have, but I'm afraid I've misplaced it. I'm sure it will turn up. Anyway, what did you think of it? It was really interesting. In fact, I think I may start an herb garden. That's exciting. But I feel bad about the book. I'll see if I can find a copy online. Please, forget about it. I'm just glad it's inspired you to give it a try yourself

I want to buy some cheese for the dinner party for the lab. Any tips? What kind of dishes are you serving? Mostly Italian. Parmesan if you're serving pasta and mozzarella if you're serving lasagna. Thanks. One of my friends who's coming is allergic to dairy products, so I'll need to figure out a substitute for her. Actually, you can get plant-based cheese in every grocery store. They make it from soy or from nuts like cashews and almonds

Miss Smith, I'm working on restocking books on the third floor in the art section and one of the shelves is in bad shape I'm worried. It's going to fall apart All right, can you clear everything off it now already did great Maintenance department so they can look at it. Those shelves have been there a long time I'm a little surprised. We haven't run into this more often

Hi, Susan you get the email from our professor about the system upgrade. Yes, I did It says the upgrade will happen overnight on Saturday, right? They mentioned that we might experience some downtime on Sunday morning. Okay. Well Most people won't be working in the lab then. Do we need to back up our files? Yes IT recommended backing up everything before the weekend. Good to know. I'll make sure to do that

Check out these photos. I took at the game last night. What do you think for a first attempt? Well, they're a little bit blurry. I'm not really sure what I'm looking at. Oh I suppose you're right. I really need to improve my photography skills Don't you join the photography club? We're happy to have members of all experience levels Have you found it helpful? You've seen the pictures from my vacation Listen to a school radio announcement Attention students we apologize for the inconvenience by the recent changes to our work-study program application process Unfortunately due to system issues some applications were not processed properly We are working to resolve this problem as quickly as possible Please resubmit your applications through the updated portal and phone the career center if you encounter any further issues Additionally, we're pleased to announce several new part-time work opportunities available on campus Feel free to stop by the center to learn more

Hey, did you see that documentary on urban development last night the one we were assigned to watch? It was way more interesting than I expected Yes, the part about how cities are redesigning public spaces was fascinating Have a hard time getting through it. It makes me look forward to our field trip to the city even more I mean, I've been a million times, but I know what you mean after this and the things our professor will probably point out It'll be a totally new experience

Hi Olga, this is Jeremy from the student affairs office. I'm calling to check on how your first been Switching universities can be challenging Thank you Overall things have been going well and my classes are interesting Oh, and I found a part-time job at a cafe in town Great. Are there any challenges you've encountered? Honestly, I'm having an issue with parking. I got assigned to lot B and it isn't anywhere near my classes That's not convenient. Let me reach out to someone in the transportation office

You know about the fundraiser for our art club, right? The one we'll do at the University Art Museum? Of course. Anything I can do to help? Actually, yes. Since you work at the museum, would you like to be the liaison between our art club and the museum? Absolutely. Out of curiosity, will you be providing food to the event guests? Well, I was hoping to spend less than a couple hundred dollars for the event, or else it'll defeat its fundraising purpose. Many caterers offer different packages. You could just order light snacks rather than full meals

I saw the new painting exhibition in the Art Museum. Have you? Moving to my new apartment has taken up all of my free time recently. There's so much packing and unpacking to do. How's the exhibit? There's a good variety of paintings, and their arrangement is really creative. I just wish it featured some stuff from artists in our area. We have so many talented painters here whose work is worth seeing. But all in all, it's quite awesome. I'll go check it out this weekend before it closes. I should be all moved in by then

I'm planning a trip to Europe next month. Great. Make sure to book your accommodations in advance and check for any travel restrictions. That's helpful. What about transportation within cities? When I visited Los Angeles in the United States last year, I had a lot of trouble getting around. Europe is different. Public transportation in most cities there is plentiful and cheap

Any fun plans for the weekend, Matt? Yeah, my cousin's getting married this weekend, actually. I need a new suit for the wedding, so I'm going to go look today after class. You should check out that new boutique on Elm Street. They have a great selection and they offer custom tailoring. That sounds perfect. How are the prices? Pretty reasonable, I think. My brother needed a suit for job interview and that's where he went

Have you heard about the concert that the University Orchestra is putting on in the park? I know you're a big fan of classical music. I am. And I'm already going with a group of friends. We're all going to bring blankets and just sit on the grass a little farther back. Good idea. I'll bring a blanket, too

This is Jimmy Clerkin from Rise Bakery, calling. I received a message that you'd like to do a story about my shop for your university newspaper. Yes, you've been a staple in our city for generations now. The grandparents of our current students were customers there when they were young. It's a family-owned business and we're proud of it. Would you be able to come next Monday at three o'clock? Definitely. Is it all right if my photographer comes along? Of course. You can get different shots, even back in our... where we do all the baking

No, not necessarily. There are members of all skill levels and everyone's very supportive. When do they meet? Every Saturday afternoon at the student center. Okay, I usually work at the library then, but they're very flexible. I'm sure I could make it work

Did you visit the amusement park during spring break? I did. The grounds were clean and the rides were fun. But I had to wait in line for half an hour just to be told they had run out of ice cream. That last part wasn't my experience. I did hear they hired a new vendor to run concessions this year though. Actually, the entire park is under new management. Give them some time to get settled in. There's an adjustment period

I need to buy a birthday present for my friend who loves cooking. Do you have any ideas? How about a high quality set of kitchen knives? That's a good idea, but I think she has a good set of knives already. She cooks a lot, so she has all the basic equipment. Hmm. Maybe you should stop by the kitchenware store downtown and ask their advice. I bet they have a lot of fun stuff for people who already have a well-stocked kitchen

I'm off to lunch. I think I'll try out that new sushi restaurant. Really? I was there just last night. The sushi was incredibly fresh and tasty. Awesome. I love good sushi. The service left a lot to be desired, but the food is definitely worth it. Tell me what you think about it. I sure will

Hi Dr Chin. I know you usually don't have office hours on Fridays, so thanks for your flexibility. I know how hectic things can get, especially at the end of the semester. So I'm interested in writing my paper about Piaget and the stages of child development. But I just wanted to make sure that topic would be okay. Well, it's pretty broad. It's only a five page paper, so I'd pick one stage instead of trying to cover all of them. That way you'll have time to get into details and really delve into the research

Have you checked out the new restaurant downtown? The sushi is made by an award-winning chef and is absolutely top notch. I'd love to, but I'm saving money for a vacation. Do you have specific plans? Not yet, but I'd love to spend a month somewhere in nature. Have you considered a camping trip? You could just pack a tent and a sleeping bag into your car and travel all around the mountains while sleeping at campsites. No need for hotels or plane tickets. Well, I do love me a comfortable bed at the end of an eventful day

How's your job at the campus bookstore going? Pretty good. It was really hectic last week, which is no surprise because the semester is just starting. Right. I actually avoided all that by ordering my books online. It was a smart move. Things have calmed down, thankfully. I think this actually might be my last semester at the bookstore, though. I heard that the tutoring center is looking for biology tutors. That's my major, so

Hi. I need to return this sweater. I bought it yesterday, but it's too small for me. Sure. Do you have the receipt? Yes. I have it right here. Great. We can process the return. Do you want to exchange it for a larger size or get a refund? I'd like to exchange it, please. No problem. I'll get another in that color for you from our stock. Thank you

Have you gone out to get the food and drinks we need for the astronomy club meeting tomorrow? It's our first meeting of the year. Not yet. I've been working on setting up the outdoor space behind the student center for our stargazing activities. There was more to do than I thought. That actually works out perfectly. I've got a few more things to add to the list. All right. Jot them down, and I'll take care of that now

I'm planning a dinner party and need to find a good bakery for desserts. Have you tried Buttercup Lane on Sargent Street? I have, but it was disappointing. Smaller portions, higher prices, and not as tasty. Did they get a new chef or change owners? Not that I know of, but they've replaced some longtime favorites, like no more chocolate mousse cake. It was always a hit at my parties. That's too bad. A couple of my guests are picky about desserts and one already asked what I'm serving. I'll need to keep looking

Caroline, I've got a question for you. Sure, Professor. I'm not sure if you're aware, but the university is redesigning its website and I'm involved in the process for the academic pages. That's great. It's long overdue if you ask me. I agree. We're looking for some students to be testers for us. Try out the new site, point out things you like and things that aren't working properly. Make suggestions, those sorts of things. Would you be interested? I don't see why not

Well, let's say I'm not going to have anyone try them. I managed to eat a couple, but only because I have strong teeth. You'll do better next time. I hope so. In fact, I'm going to try again tonight. I'm headed to the store right now to get some extra flour and sugar

In theory, that sounds great, but I need to make money now. I went to the bookstore yesterday and spent way more than I had expected

Hmm, I'll think about it. I have a lot of studying to do for my law school exam, but I'd love to see the performance of the headline band, The Rumbling Waves. Why weren't you at the book reading at the library yesterday? I wanted to go, but I got stuck helping out with a volunteer event. I didn't know you were still doing that. Only sometimes. Hi, Lisa. Have you managed to set up the new software on your computer? Not yet. I tried installing it, but I keep getting an error message. Did you check if your system meets the software requirements? Yes, my computer is definitely compatible, so I think it might be a network issue. You should contact IT support. They can help you sort it out. Good idea. I'll give them a call this afternoon. Let me know how it goes

My printer isn't working and I have to print this report now. What's the rush? I have class at three o'clock. Have you checked if it's out of paper or ink? Yes, I did. The paper tray is full and I replaced the ink cartridge yesterday. Maybe it's a software issue. You could try reinstalling the printer driver. That often resolves these kinds of problems. Or you can call the help desk. That will take forever and it's almost three o'clock. I guess I'll just use the printer in the computer lab

Have you tried that new Mediterranean restaurant on Maple Street? I went there last night with my sister. The food was fantastic, especially the grilled fish. But there was a bit of a hiccup. What happened? Well, we had a reservation for seven o'clock, but they didn't seat us until almost 7.45. Apparently, the kitchen was short staffed and running behind. That's frustrating. Did the staff handle it well? They were apologetic and even offered us complementary appetizers while we waited. Sounds like they made up for it. I've been meaning to check it out

I think we need to meet again to discuss the group project. Definitely. Still a lot to talk about. Are you free Thursday afternoon? We can meet at the cafe near the library. Does Friday work for you? I don't have classes on Thursdays, so I'm not planning to come over to campus. Sure, but I thought you lived in a dorm on campus. I did, but I just moved last weekend. I still have lots of boxes piled up in my new apartment. Moving is always a lot of work

I heard that the university is sponsoring a creative writing contest. You should enter. Me? No way. There are going to be so many more talented people entering. Don't be so modest. What about that story you wrote last year for your English class? Didn't you say your teacher raved about it? Yes, but, well, I'll think about it. I'm not working at the bookstore anymore, so I can't use the excuse that I'm too busy

I'm excited our university is merging with Clark University. There'll be more classes to choose from and even some new degree programs. I've heard they're thinking about eliminating some majors that only have a small number of students. And right now, we only have 10 students in my program. I wasn't aware of that. Hopefully they won't get rid of yours. Yeah, if they do, I'll have to think about transferring elsewhere to finish my degree

So have you started planning your vacation yet? Yes, I started looking into train tickets yesterday. Train tickets? But that would take you about 10 hours to get to Chicago. Why not fly? Well, I want to enjoy the scenery along the way. I see, and you're going for one week, right? Two weeks, actually. That means you'll be getting back to town right before next semester starts. I know, I'm going to get everything in order before I leave, though. That should help me avoid stressing out when I get back

Hey, I'm getting bored listening to the same old pop singers. Any recommendations for me? You came to the right guy. You know I'm always on the lookout for the next big thing. I've really been into this punk rock band lately. Sounds fun, but I'm looking for something more mellow. Well, I was on a date at the cafe last night, and there was a jazz quartet playing. Their singer was a smooth baritone. I think she used to be an opera singer. If you're looking for something relaxed, you'll love her

Have you heard about the concert that the University Orchestra is putting on in the park? I know you're a big fan of classical music I am and I'm already going with a group of friends. You should come with us. Thanks. I'd love to So how much are tickets? You only have to pay if you sit in the seats up close near the stage We're all going to bring blankets and just sit on the grass a little farther back. Good idea. I'll bring a blanket, too

Did you see the announcement for the new book club on campus? I did it's interesting They're reading a mix of poetry and modern fiction and once a month They're going to invite authors to discuss their works. I've always wanted to hear authors talk about their writing process Same here. Are you thinking what I'm thinking? I am. Let's do it

I'm excited our university is merging with university There'll be more classes to choose from and even some new degree programs I've heard they're thinking about eliminating some majors that only have a small number of students and Right now we only have 10 students in my program. I wasn't aware of that Hopefully they won't get rid of yours. Yeah, if they do, I'll have to think about transferring elsewhere to finish my degree

Hey, did you see that documentary on urban development development last night the one we were assigned to watch? It was way more interesting than I expected Yes, the part about how cities are redesigning public spaces was fascinating I thought I'd have a hard time getting through it. It makes me look forward to our field trip to the city even more I mean, I've been a million times, but I know what you mean After this and the things our professor will probably point out It'll be a totally new experience Listen to what say Hey, Sam Rob was telling me you've taken up painting. I've always thought about it, but I'm not sure I'm good enough, you know talented enough That was the same for me. It's why it took me so long to get started But I'm glad I did You just take your time with it. Go at your own pace Have you taken any classes in the art department? Honestly, I've just been watching some online tutorials nothing formal Yesterday, I finished a landscape with mountains a lake and trees Great artist yet, but maybe someday

I need to improve my fitness. And I saw the physical education department is offering water aerobics classes next term. I hope the timing works out because those sound like a lot of fun. Well, just to warn you, I strained my back a little doing aquatic jumping jacks a few years ago. Oh no! Have you recovered? Yes, it wasn't that bad and it hasn't scared me away from water. I swim laps to stay in shape. I hadn't thought about swimming laps. That sounds even better

I'm thinking about joining a gym. Do you have any suggestions? Yes, the fitness factory on Main Street is really good. They have a variety of classes and modern equipment. That sounds great. Do they offer personal training sessions? Yes, they do. The trainers are very capable. Perfect. I'll check it out this week

Where are you off to? We're about to try the new cafe next to the bookstore. Have you been there yet? No, is it any good? They have the most amazing pastries. Good coffee? Of course. I was just on my way to the library. I have to review my notes before class

Are you still working on that project for your ecology class? Yes, but I'm almost finished. I just have to present my findings to the class next week. I see. Well, I'm glad I'm not in your shoes. Talking in front of people makes me nervous. Really? I had an internship as a tour guide at Dawson State Park last summer, so I think I can handle this. Oh, okay. Speaking of internships, I'm still waiting to hear back from the History Museum downtown. I set my application in last month, but I still haven't heard anything

I want to buy some cheese for the dinner party for the lab. Any tips? What kind of dishes are you serving? Mostly Italian. I'd get Parmesan if you're serving pasta and mozzarella if you're serving lasagna. Thanks. One of my friends who's coming is allergic to dairy products, so I'll need to figure out a substitute for her. Actually, you can get plant-based cheese in every grocery store. They make it from soy or from nuts like cashews and almonds

Did you see the announcement for the new book club on campus? I did. It sounds interesting. They're reading a mix of poetry and modern fiction. And once a month they're going to invite authors to discuss their works. I've always wanted to hear authors talk about their writing process. Same here. Are you thinking what I'm thinking? I am. Let's do it

Have you heard about the concert that the University Orchestra is putting on in the park? I know you're a big fan of classical music. I am. And I'm already going with a group of friends. You should come with us. Thanks. I'd love to. So how much are tickets? You only have to pay if you sit in the seats up close near the stage. We're all going to bring tickets and just sit on the grass a little farther back. Good idea. I'll bring a blank too

I just interviewed the director of campus housing for the university newspaper and she confirmed the rumors. The cost to live in the dormitories will be about 10% higher next year. Oh wow. That's a lot. I'm wondering if it would make sense to start looking for an apartment or something that's not on campus. That's what I am planning to do. If I come across any good ones I'm not taking, I'll pass them along to you. Awesome. Thanks

Excuse me? How may I help you? My brother's getting a cooking lately, but I don't know what to get him. What do you suggest? How about a high quality chef's knife? We have a lot of options at different price points. I don't know if he needs one. I think he already has knives. Most home cooks start with a basic setup. A more professional knife makes food prep a lot easier, and it's something that most people wouldn't buy for themselves. I really want to treat him. How about that one?

Hey Sam, Rob was telling me you've taken up painting. I've always thought about it, but I'm not sure I'm good enough. You know, talented enough. That was the same for me. It's why it took me so long to get started, but I'm glad I did. You just take your time with it. Go at your own pace. Have you taken any classes in the art department? Honestly, I've just been watching some online tutorials. Nothing formal. Yesterday, I finished a landscape with mountains, a lake, and trees. I'm not a great artist yet, but maybe someday

Are you going to the outdoor concert this Saturday? Is it still happening with all the rain in the forecast? They said it's on, rain or shine. Plus, we might get a dry window in the evening. Hmm, I'll think about it. I have a lot of studying to do for my law school exam, but I'd love to see the performance of the headline band, The Rumbling Waves. I grew up in the same neighborhood as their lead singer and even played basketball with him a few times

Why weren't you at the book reading at the library yesterday? I wanted to go, but I got stuck helping out with a volunteer event. I didn't know you were still doing that. Only sometimes. The animal shelter really needed help. I think I'm going to take myself off the volunteer list, though. Well, the featured author yesterday was Courtney Reimer, and she mostly talked about her new book, The Breath of Life. I'm sorry I missed it. The only downside was that the question and answer session was so short. Only two or three people had a chance to ask her anything

Did you visit the amusement park during spring break? I did. The grounds were clean and the rides were fun. But I had to wait in line for half an hour just to be told they had run out of ice cream. That last part wasn't my experience. I did hear they hired a new vendor to run concessions this year, though. Actually, the entire park is under new management. Give them some time to get settled in. There's an adjustment period. Listen to a school radio announcement. Good afternoon, students. We're excited to invite you to the annual university student achievement award ceremony, taking place on Friday at 5pm in the Baker Auditorium. We'll be honoring recipients of the Research Excellence Award, the Student Leadership Award, and the Community Engagement Fellowship. You'll hear inspiring stories from students who've made a real difference on campus and beyond. There will be a short reception afterward with refreshments and a chance to meet faculty, staff and fellow students

Have you tried the yoga class at the University Recreation Centre? No, I haven't. But I've been meaning to. I really like the morning class. It's at 8am with an instructor named Julie. I'll look into it. The only issue involves space. They have us packed so tightly in that room that you can barely move. Well, they must offer more than one session, right? Yeah, there's an evening one too. But it meets at the same time as my biology seminar

Have you checked out the new restaurant downtown? The sushi is made by an award-winning chef and is absolutely top-notch. I'd love to, but I'm saving money for a vacation. Do you have specific plans? Not yet, but I'd love to spend a month somewhere in nature. Have you considered a camping trip? You could just pack a tent and a sleeping bag into your car and travel all around the mountains while sleeping at campsites. No need for hotels or plane tickets. Well, I do love me a comfortable bed at the end of an eventful day

Did you start your part-time job at the potato chip factory in town yet? I did. I like it, though it'll be a lot to balance with classes. My first day was quite interesting. Oh? My job was to take the bags of chips and put them in their shipping boxes. Okay. Which was fine, but then the machine that puts the chips into the bags went haywire. Half the chips weren't making it in. Oh wow, that definitely qualifies as an interesting first day

Have you gone out to get the food and drinks we need for the astronomy club meeting tomorrow? It's our first meeting of the year. Not yet. I've been working on setting up the outdoor space behind the student center for our stargazing activities. There was more to do than I thought. That actually works out perfectly. I've got a few more things to add to the list. All right, jot them down, and I'll take care of that now

Hi Susan, did you get the email from our professor about the system upgrade? Yes, I did. It says the upgrade will happen overnight on Saturday. Right. They mentioned that we might experience some downtime on Sunday morning. Okay, well, most people won't be working in the lab then. Do we need to back up our files? Yes, IT recommended backing up everything before the weekend. Good to know. I'll make sure to do that

I'm planning a dinner party and need to find a good bakery for desserts. Have you tried Buttercup Lane on Sargent Street? I have, but it was disappointing. Smaller portions, higher prices, and not as tasty. Did they get a new chef or change owners? Not that I know of, but they've replaced some longtime favorites, like no more chocolate mousse cake. It was always a hit at my parties. That's too bad. A couple of my guests are picky about desserts and one already asked what I'm serving. I'll need to keep looking

Caroline, I've got a question for you. Sure, Professor. I'm not sure if you're aware, but the university is redesigning its website, and I'm involved in the process for the academic pages. That's great. It's long overdue if you ask me. I agree. We're looking for some students to be testers for us. Try out the new site, point out things you like and things that aren't working properly, make suggestions, those sorts of things. Would you be interested? I don't see why not
